title: server.rs: handle_inbox Follow fetches follower actor doc without size check on HTTP status
description:
LOCATION: src/server.rs:961-983, handle_inbox() Follow arm

FINDING: When processing a Follow activity, the server fetches the follower's actor document (line 962-983). If the fetch returns a non-success status, the code returns StatusCode::ACCEPTED (line 982), silently accepting the Follow without actually storing the follower's inbox. The follower is then inserted with an empty inbox_uri (line 985: let inbox_uri = actor_doc["inbox"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string()). 

However the code checks for empty inbox_uri at line 1003 and returns ACCEPTED without inserting. So an actor whose document is temporarily unavailable causes a silent Follow loss — the follower is never stored and the Accept is never sent, but the follower's server sees a 202 and considers the follow accepted. The follower never receives posts.

IMPACT: If a remote server is temporarily slow or flaky at Follow time, the follow relationship is silently lost — neither an error nor a stored pending state. The follower sees their follow accepted but receives no posts, with no way to detect or recover without re-following.
close_reason: Fixed: Follow actor doc fetch failure returns 500, not silent 202
